title: salvia × sylvestris subsp. tesquicola
intro: Salvia × sylvestris subsp. tesquicola, also known as the woodland sage, is a subspecies of Salvia sylvestris that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Salvia × sylvestris subsp. tesquicola include relief from respiratory issues, improved cognitive function, and reduced stress levels. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be used fresh in herbal remedies or dried for later use.
